research.name: Microsoft Corporation
research.description: Microsoft Corporation is an American multinational technology company which produces computer software, consumer electronics, personal computers, and related services. Its best known software products are the Microsoft Windows line of operating systems, the Microsoft Office suite, and the Internet Explorer and Edge web browsers. Its flagship hardware products are the Xbox video game consoles and the Microsoft Surface lineup of touchscreen personal computers. Microsoft ranked No. 21 in the 2020 Fortune 500 rankings of the largest United States corporations by total revenue; it was the world's largest software maker by revenue as of 2016. It is considered one of the Big Five companies in the U.S. information technology industry, along with Google, Apple, Amazon, and Facebook.
strengths: Strong profitability and cash generation score (76/100)

research.name: Alphabet Inc Class A
research.description: Alphabet Inc. is an American multinational conglomerate headquartered in Mountain View, California. It was created through a restructuring of Google on October 2, 2015, and became the parent company of Google and several former Google subsidiaries. The two co-founders of Google remained as controlling shareholders, board members, and employees at Alphabet. Alphabet is the world's fourth-largest technology company by revenue and one of the world's most valuable companies.
strengths: Strong profitability and cash generation score (76/100)

news.title: SLB Announces Second-Quarter 2026 Results
news.summary: SLB announced its second-quarter 2026 results, reporting revenue of $8.97 billion, a 3% sequential and 5% year-on-year increase. GAAP EPS was $0.52, and adjusted EBITDA reached $1.90 billion, rising 7% sequentially. The company highlighted strong sequential growth in international markets, driven by offshore activity and a rebound in U.S. unconventionals, which largely offset disruptions in the Middle East.

news.title: Why T-Mobile Stock Dropped 11% Despite a Q2 Earnings Beat
news.summary: T-Mobile's stock dropped 11% despite beating Q2 earnings estimates on key profit metrics, primarily due to a warning about elevated Q3 net postpaid account churn. Management attributes this temporary churn to a strategic rate plan modernization, which they expect will lead to long-term pricing power and customer lifetime value improvements through 2027. TIKR's model projects a significant 80% return for TMUS stock by 2030, based on expanding margins, disciplined capital return, and anticipated recovery from the current strategic reset.

news.title: Inceptionr LLC Purchases 23,020 Shares of Bank of America Corporation $BAC
news.summary: Inceptionr LLC significantly increased its holdings in Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) by 215.2% during the first quarter, purchasing an additional 23,020 shares. The firm now owns 33,718 shares valued at $1,644,000. Other institutional investors like Norges Bank and Vanguard Group Inc. have also made substantial moves in BAC stock, with notable insider selling by Geoffrey S. Greener also reported.

news.title: Public Employees Retirement System of Ohio Reduces Stake in Welltower Inc. $WELL
news.summary: The Public Employees Retirement System of Ohio reduced its stake in Welltower Inc. (NYSE:WELL) by 12.3% in the first quarter, selling 149,580 shares but still holding over 1 million shares. This comes as Welltower reported strong quarterly results, beating analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and reaffirmed its FY 2026 EPS guidance. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Welltower, with several firms raising price targets.

news.title: Mizuho Markets Americas LLC Sells 94,274 Shares of Analog Devices, Inc. $ADI
news.summary: Mizuho Markets Americas LLC has significantly reduced its stake in Analog Devices, Inc. by selling 94,274 shares, decreasing its holdings by 29.9% to 220,726 shares valued at $70.22 million. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions, while company insiders, including CEO Vincent Roche and SVP Martin Cotter, have sold substantial amounts of their stock. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for ADI with a consensus price target of $441.00.

news.title: Amazon FTC settlement deadline is just days away. How to file claim
news.summary: Eligible Amazon customers have until Monday, July 27, to file a claim in the $2.5 billion settlement with the Federal Trade Commission. This settlement addresses allegations that Amazon coerced consumers into Prime subscriptions and made cancellations difficult. Customers who signed up for Prime between June 23, 2019, and June 23, 2025, and unsuccessfully tried to cancel or enrolled through "challenged enrollment flows," may receive up to $51, with payments expected by September.

news.title: The Walt Disney Company $DIS Shares Acquired by Sei Investments Co.
news.summary: Sei Investments Co. significantly increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company, acquiring an additional 304,662 shares, bringing its total to over 1 million shares valued at approximately $97.7 million. Despite general positive sentiment from Wall Street with a "Moderate Buy" rating, Disney's stock is near its 52-week low due to recent layoffs, analyst caution, and weak share performance, even though its latest quarterly results surpassed earnings and revenue expectations. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings in Disney, reflecting ongoing market dynamics for the entertainment giant.

news.title: Lido Advisors LLC Purchases 24,281 Shares of Texas Pacific Land Corporation $TPL
news.summary: Lido Advisors LLC significantly increased its stake in Texas Pacific Land Corporation, purchasing an additional 24,281 shares to bring its total holding to 44,013 shares valued at approximately $20.9 million. This increase follows strong quarterly results from Texas Pacific Land, which exceeded analyst expectations for EPS and revenue. Despite the positive financial performance and a declared dividend, analysts maintain a "Reduce" consensus rating for the stock with a target price of $639.00.

news.title: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. Has $51.02 Million Stock Holdings in Analog Devices, Inc. $ADI
news.summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. increased its stake in Analog Devices, Inc. by 2.8% in the first quarter, holding 160,374 shares valued at over $51 million. This increase follows strong quarterly results from Analog Devices, which surpassed EPS and revenue estimates, and the company continues to receive positive analyst ratings and a regular quarterly dividend. Several other institutional investors also modified their positions in Analog Devices, with some insiders selling shares.

news.title: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. Sells 6,143 Shares of Texas Pacific Land Corporation $TPL
news.summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. reduced its stake in Texas Pacific Land Corporation by 4.0% in the first quarter, selling 6,143 shares and retaining 146,982 shares valued at approximately $69.8 million. Despite a recent earnings beat and dividend payment, the company holds a consensus "Reduce" rating from analysts with an average target price of $639. The article also details other institutional investor activity and recent insider stock sales.

news.title: Meeder Asset Management Inc. Buys 4,641 Shares of Alphabet Inc. $GOOG
news.summary: Meeder Asset Management Inc. increased its stake in Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOG) by 1.4% in the first quarter, purchasing an additional 4,641 shares to bring its total holdings to 339,260 shares, valued at approximately $97.3 million. This makes Alphabet its third-largest holding. The company reported strong Q2 earnings, beating revenue and EPS expectations, though the stock saw a decline despite a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts.

news.title: SEB Asset Management AB Makes New Investment in monday.com Ltd. $MNDY
news.summary: SEB Asset Management AB has acquired a new position in monday.com Ltd. (NASDAQ:MNDY), purchasing 23,710 shares valued at approximately $1,639,000 during the first quarter. Other institutional investors like Swiss National Bank, Aurora Investment Counsel, Aware Super Pty Ltd, Sequoia Financial Advisors LLC, and Hsbc Holdings PLC have also adjusted their holdings in the company. The article also provides an overview of monday.com's recent trading performance, earnings, and analyst sentiment, noting the company's recent restructuring around AI and related workforce reductions.

news.title: Why Is Alphabet (GOOGL) Still Falling After a Record Q2 Beat?
news.summary: Alphabet's shares have fallen despite a strong Q2 earnings beat, driven by record capital expenditure in AI infrastructure, a new $1 billion EU regulatory fine, and headline EPS inflation from one-time investment gains. Despite these concerns, 63 analysts maintain a "Buy" rating with an average $417 target, citing solid fundamental performance and an oversold technical position. The market remains divided on whether the massive AI investments will yield sufficient future operating margins.

news.title: Torren Management LLC Sells 3,414 Shares of Apple Inc. $AAPL
news.summary: Torren Management LLC significantly reduced its stake in Apple Inc. ($AAPL) by 78.8% in the first quarter, selling 3,414 shares and retaining 918 shares valued at approximately $233,000. Despite this sale, Apple reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with an EPS of $2.01 and revenue of $111.18 billion, and also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.27 per share. Analyst sentiment remains largely positive, maintaining a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $326.83, even as some market notes point to a rich valuation.

news.title: Compound Planning Inc. Sells 13,231 Shares of Bank of America Corporation $BAC
news.summary: Compound Planning Inc. has reduced its stake in Bank of America Corporation (NYSE:BAC) by 7.5% in the first quarter, selling 13,231 shares and now owning 163,030 shares valued at $7.95 million. Despite this reduction, several other institutional investors, including Vanguard Group Inc., Norges Bank, and Bank of New York Mellon Corp, increased their holdings in Bank of America during the fourth quarter. Analysts have issued "Buy" or "Overweight" ratings for BAC, with a consensus price target of $63.77.

news.title: Campbell's swaps red labels for green on new soups
news.summary: Campbell's is introducing a new line of five protein-rich soups with green-and-white labels, a significant change from their traditional red-and-white design used for over 125 years. This shift aims to capitalize on the growing consumer demand for high-protein foods, with the new soups featuring slow-simmered bone broth and other protein sources. The soups are currently available on Amazon.com and are rolling out to retailers nationwide.

news.title: Why this money manager added to his Paycom position and sold a Japanese consumer review stock
news.summary: Money manager Jason Del Vicario, overseeing $280-million in assets for Hillside Wealth Management, has been actively finding investment opportunities despite market overvaluation. He focuses on high-quality companies with strong management and consistent returns, adding to positions like Copart, Kinsale Capital Group, and Paycom Software, while selling Kakaku.com Inc. due to a private equity takeover. Del Vicario's strategy emphasizes long-term holdings and buying when prices are favorable, even if it means underperforming AI-driven market surges in the short term.

news.title: Nwam LLC Buys 8,055 Shares of Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL
news.summary: Nwam LLC increased its stake in Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) by 19.6% in the first quarter, adding 8,055 shares to hold a total of 49,128 shares valued at $14.1 million. Despite a strong earnings beat with significant revenue and EPS growth, investors are wary of Alphabet's rising capital expenditures for AI infrastructure and regulatory pressures, including a new €890 million EU antitrust fine. The company also recently declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share.

news.title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL Shares Sold by Legacy Capital Group California Inc.
news.summary: Legacy Capital Group California Inc. reduced its stake in Alphabet (GOOGL) by 26.8% in the first quarter, selling 3,724 shares but still holding over 10,000 shares valued at $2.93 million. This comes despite strong earnings for Alphabet, which reported revenue of $119.8 billion and EPS of $9.11, both exceeding expectations. However, investor concerns about increased AI-related capital spending, negative free cash flow, and a new €890 million EU antitrust fine are putting pressure on the stock.

news.title: Microsoft And Meta Earnings Previews
news.summary: The article discusses the earnings previews for Microsoft and Meta Platforms, focusing on their capital expenditure (CapEx) for AI infrastructure. Consensus projections indicate a significant increase in CapEx for 2026, with Microsoft's CapEx expected to nearly triple by FY 2027 and Meta's rising by over $10 billion for FY 2026. The market is keenly watching whether these companies can maintain their CapEx guidance while ensuring profitability amidst aggressive AI investments.

news.title: The Walt Disney Company $DIS Stock Holdings Increased by Gibbs Wealth Management
news.summary: Gibbs Wealth Management significantly increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 111.8% in the first quarter, now holding 26,143 shares valued at over $2.5 million. Other institutional investors also raised their positions, indicating sustained large-scale interest, despite the stock trading near its 52-week low amidst layoffs and analyst price target adjustments. Analysts currently rate DIS as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $129.00.

news.summary: Bleakley Financial Group LLC increased its stake in Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) by 8.7% in the first quarter, bringing their total holdings to $57,855,000. news.title: ABC Arbitrage SA Acquires Shares of 4,494 Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL
